verses.1: Praise the LORD! Oh give thanks to the LORD, for He is good; For His mercy is everlasting.
verses.2: Who can speak of the mighty deeds of the LORD, Or can proclaim all His praise?
verses.3: How blessed are those who maintain justice, Who practice righteousness at all times!
verses.4: Remember me, LORD, in Your favor toward Your people. Visit me with Your salvation,
verses.5: So that I may see the prosperity of Your chosen ones, That I may rejoice in the joy of Your nation, That I may boast with Your inheritance.
verses.6: We have sinned like our fathers, We have gone astray, we have behaved wickedly.
verses.7: Our fathers in Egypt did not understand Your wonders; They did not remember Your abundant kindnesses, But rebelled by the sea, at the Red Sea.
verses.8: Nevertheless He saved them for the sake of His name, So that He might make His power known.
verses.9: So He rebuked the Red Sea and it dried up, And He led them through the mighty waters, as through the wilderness.
verses.10: So He saved them from the hand of one who hated them, And redeemed them from the hand of the enemy.
verses.11: The waters covered their adversaries; Not one of them was left.
verses.12: Then they believed His words; They sang His praise.
verses.13: They quickly forgot His works; They did not wait for His plan,
verses.14: But became lustfully greedy in the wilderness, And put God to the test in the desert.
verses.15: So He gave them their request, But sent a wasting disease among them.
verses.16: When they became envious of Moses in the camp, And of Aaron, the holy one of the LORD,
verses.17: The earth opened and swallowed up Dathan, And engulfed the company of Abiram.
verses.18: And a fire blazed up in their company; The flame consumed the wicked.
verses.19: They made a calf in Horeb, And worshiped a cast metal image.
verses.20: So they exchanged their glory For the image of an ox that eats grass.
verses.21: They forgot God their Savior, Who had done great things in Egypt,
verses.22: Wonders in the land of Ham, And awesome things by the Red Sea.
verses.23: Therefore He said that He would destroy them, If Moses, His chosen one, had not stood in the gap before Him, To turn away His wrath from destroying them.
verses.24: Then they rejected the pleasant land; They did not believe His word,
verses.25: But grumbled in their tents; They did not listen to the voice of the LORD.
verses.26: Therefore He swore to them That He would have them fall in the wilderness,
verses.27: And that He would bring down their descendants among the nations, And scatter them in the lands.
verses.28: They also followed Baal-peor, And ate sacrifices offered to the dead.
verses.29: So they provoked Him to anger with their deeds, And a plague broke out among them.
verses.30: Then Phinehas stood up and intervened, And so the plague was brought to a halt.
verses.31: And it was credited to him as righteousness, To all generations forever.
verses.32: They also provoked Him to wrath at the waters of Meribah, So that it went badly for Moses on their account.
verses.33: Because they were rebellious against His Spirit, He spoke rashly with his lips.
verses.34: They did not destroy the peoples, As the LORD had commanded them,
verses.35: But they got involved with the nations And learned their practices,
verses.36: And served their idols, Which became a snare to them.
verses.37: They even sacrificed their sons and their daughters to the demons,
verses.38: And shed innocent blood, The blood of their sons and their daughters Whom they sacrificed to the idols of Canaan; And the land was defiled with the blood.
verses.39: So they became unclean in their practices, And were unfaithful in their deeds.
verses.40: Therefore the anger of the LORD was kindled against His people, And He loathed His inheritance.
verses.41: So He handed them over to the nations, And those who hated them ruled over them.
verses.42: Their enemies also oppressed them, And they were subdued under their power.
verses.43: Many times He would rescue them; They, however, were rebellious in their plan, And they sank down into their guilt.
verses.44: Nevertheless He looked at their distress When He heard their cry;
verses.45: And He remembered His covenant for their sake, And relented according to the greatness of His mercy.
verses.46: He also made them objects of compassion In the presence of all their captors.
verses.47: Save us, LORD our God, And gather us from the nations, To give thanks to Your holy name And glory in Your praise.
verses.48: Blessed be the LORD, the God of Israel, From everlasting to everlasting. And all the people shall say, “Amen.” Praise the LORD!
